steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: Before the blank: She was not alone in her efforts — a complete clause. After the blank: Fernando Orejuela and Stephanie Shonekan, among other scholars, also contributed to the project — another complete clause. The conjunctive adverb however sits between them.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation pattern
steps.content:
Per the answer key, the right pattern is efforts, however; — a comma before however and a semicolon after to join the next independent clause.

Choice B — efforts however omits all punctuation, producing a run-on; Choice C — efforts: however, uses a colon before however, which doesn't fit; Choice D — efforts, however, (comma–however–comma) creates a comma splice between two complete clauses.

The answer is A.
